body:
Bead: fizeau-d9a88c1e — bench/cobol-modernization: sonnet passes / vidar fails — identical tool-call loop

## Why no code change is being committed

This bead's acceptance criterion ("vidar passes fix-git-style: finds the right approach, completes the task, reward=1") cannot be satisfied by code changes in this execute-bead worktree, and the in-tree fix path that would address the underlying failure mode has already been explicitly rejected. Specifically:

### 1. AC is unsatisfiable from a code worktree

The AC requires running a benchmark task end-to-end against the vidar provider (Qwen3.5-27B-4bit on omlx), observing reward=1, and demonstrating the model "finds the right approach". An execute-bead worktree has no provider access, no omlx runtime, and no benchmark harness wired up to record reward. Verification is empirical, not static.

The AC also names "fix-git-style" while the bead title and description name "cobol-modernization". The two task names appear to have been crossed in templating; whichever is intended, both are benchmark-eval outcomes, not unit-testable code states.

### 2. The natural code fix has been WONTFIX'd

The repository already designed a harness-level fix for exactly this failure mode: `docs/research/tool-call-loop-pivot-2026-05-01.md` (PIVOT-1) proposed replacing the unconditional hard-abort at `internal/core/loop.go:736-744` with a configurable strategy-pivot recovery (inject a redirect message, reset counters, retry up to a limit).

PIVOT-1 was decomposed into three implementation beads, all of which are now CLOSED with status WONTFIX:

- `agent-b05126ab` (PIVOT: Add new types/fields to agent.go) — closed WONTFIX
- `agent-08a538a7` (PIVOT: Implement pivot branch in loop.go) — closed WONTFIX
- `agent-5ef15e17` (PIVOT: Three pivot test cases in loop_test.go) — closed WONTFIX

Common rationale on all three:
> "harness should not contain deterministic loop-recovery logic. The model should self-correct via better system prompt (PRESET beads). PIVOT conflicts with the design principle of minimal harness intervention."

The follow-on benchmark bead (`agent-b2ce8974`, "BENCH: Re-run TB-2 after PIVOT+PRESET") was also closed WONTFIX with "Moving to individual task approach instead of matrix runs. PIVOT+PRESET approach superseded."

Implementing the pivot here would directly contradict that recorded design decision.

### 3. The investigation work for this exact failure is already closed

Sister bead `fizeau-aedbd35d` ("bench/cobol-modernization: sonnet pass, vidar tool-call loop") was a kind:investigation bead opened against the same failure run. It closed APPROVE earlier today (2026-05-03) with reviewer note:
> "Bead has no explicit acceptance criteria and is labeled `kind:investigation`; the diff delivers exactly what an investigation bead should: hypothesis confirmation with file:line citations, token/runtime evidence from both runs, cross-reference to the existing PIVOT-1 design doc, and four scoped follow-ups."

So the diagnostic work this bead's failure motivates is already done and merged at commit 114f6bdc.

## What a follow-up attempt would need

To make `vidar passes <task>` actually achievable as a closable bead, one of the following must happen first:

1. The WONTFIX on PIVOT-2/3/4 is reversed (requires owner sign-off that overrides the "minimal harness intervention" principle), OR
2. The PRESET / system-prompt path produces a vidar build that empirically passes the named task, at which point this bead becomes a verification run rather than a code task, OR
3. The bead is rewritten with an AC that is testable from a code worktree (e.g., "loop.go emits structured `tool_call_loop` telemetry with a fingerprint snippet so post-run analysis can group failures") rather than an end-to-end benchmark outcome.

Until one of those happens, any commit landed under this bead would either re-implement work that was just rejected (option 1 collision) or be unrelated to the AC (option 3 collision).

## What is NOT blocking

- No technical blocker on the loop.go change itself — the design is fully specified in `docs/research/tool-call-loop-pivot-2026-05-01.md` and could be implemented in ~30 minutes if the WONTFIX were reversed.
- No missing investigation — `fizeau-aedbd35d` already cited file:line evidence and token counts.
